title: Skill extraction should update/merge existing skills, not only append
description: When Mira's explorer extracts a new skill from a reading or briefing, it currently creates a new skill file without checking for semantic overlap with the existing skill library. The reading note's core insight is that the bottleneck in knowledge systems is not generating new knowledge units but maintaining a stable, composable encoding — one where new knowledge updates or supersedes old encodings rather than accumulating alongside them. Concretely: add a pre-save step in the skill extraction path that (1) loads existing skill titles + tags from soul/, (2) asks the model whether the new skill overlaps with or supersedes an existing one, and (3) if overlap is found, updates the existing file rather than writing a new one, preserving the skill's filename and retrieval path. This keeps the encoding system coherent over time.
rationale: The reading note distinguishes two roles for AI: answer-generator vs. knowledge-encoding-infrastructure. Mira's current skill extraction is purely additive — each reading produces a new file appended to the library. This is the answer-generator mode. The encoding-infrastructure mode requires that new knowledge update the existing representation when it supersedes it. The note's framing — 'know-how 被稳定保存、调用和重组' (stably preserved, retrieved, and recombined) — maps directly to skill deduplication: stable means no redundant variants of the same skill drifting apart; retrievable means the canonical file stays at a known path; recombinable means skills stay coherent enough to compose without contradicting each other.
diff_description: In soul_manager.py (or wherever skills are saved), add a function `find_overlapping_skill(new_skill_title, new_skill_tags, new_skill_body) -> Optional[skill_path]` that loads the existing skill index (titles + tags from CLAUDE.md or soul/ directory), calls Claude with a short prompt asking whether the new skill substantially overlaps an existing one, and returns the path if yes. In the explorer's skill-save path, call this before writing: if overlap found, overwrite the existing file (preserving its filename) and re-sync CLAUDE.md; if no overlap, proceed with the current new-file behavior. The overlap check prompt should be tight: 'Does skill A cover the same operational territory as skill B such that having both would create contradictory or redundant guidance?' — binary yes/no with the overlapping skill path.
source_note: 2026-04-03_技术进步最让我改观的一点是它更像知识的编码系统在进化而不只是单点发明在变.md
